reason: The Actual Output demonstrates exceptional fidelity across all four evaluation dimensions. (1) Persona preservation: The primary persona (male, 28–45, shops Bonobos/Rhone/Peter Millar, wears fandom at brunch/golf/casual Friday) and secondary persona (nostalgic heritage fan, 38–52) are reproduced with full psychographic and behavioral detail, including all eight audience signals translated into actionable design and mockup constraints. (2) Strategic alignment: All four priority angles ('The Club Member, Not the Crowd,' 'Heritage Over Hype,' 'Wear It Everywhere,' 'Subtle Signal, Strong Identity') are explicitly named, explained, and embedded into design execution directives. The core message is reproduced verbatim, and all five supporting messages are reflected in the brief's tone and copy guidance. Brand fit and SKU fit rationales are faithfully translated. (3) Constraint compliance: Every must_keep element (premium feel, heritage, crest/badge, everyday wearability) is present and operationalized. Every must_avoid element (official logos, NFL marks, arrowhead, clipart, oversized placement, stadium context, streetwear aesthetic) is explicitly listed and expanded with specific forbidden elements including the exact Chiefs red hex code. IP safety notes are fully preserved and expanded. (4) Execution specificity: The brief provides exceptional downstream actionability — precise badge sizing (2.5–3 inches), two colorway specifications with palette rationale, typography direction (serif/slab-serif, arched, no sans-serif), illustration style (vintage engraving, no mascots), mockup direction per output type (flat, worn, lifestyle), model age/styling/setting specs, self-review checklist, human review flags, and open questions. The design problem statement from the strategy is directly solved. No strategic element is dropped, reordered, or contradicted.

revision_feedback.required_action: Revise POD Design Brief to resolve the alignment issues.
